panels.title: Avg Query Latency
panels.description: Shows the average latency across all database queries in the selected time range. A rising average often signals connection pool saturation or increasing query complexity.

panels.title: p95 Query Latency
panels.description: Shows the 95th percentile database query latency. This represents the worst-case experience for most users and is more sensitive to slow queries than the average.

panels.title: p99 Query Latency
panels.description: Shows the 99th percentile database query latency. Outlier queries captured here often point to missing indexes or lock contention.

panels.title: Total DB Spans
panels.description: Shows the total number of database spans observed in the selected time range. Use this to gauge overall database call volume.

panels.title: p99 Latency by System
panels.description: Tracks the 99th percentile query latency for each database system over time. Compare systems to identify which one contributes the most tail latency.

panels.title: p99 Latency by Operation
panels.description: Tracks the 99th percentile query latency grouped by operation type (SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, etc.). Watch for specific operations whose latency diverges from the baseline.

panels.title: Ops/sec by System
panels.description: Tracks the query throughput for each database system over time. Correlate throughput changes with latency shifts to distinguish load-driven slowdowns from efficiency regressions.

panels.title: Ops/sec by Operation
panels.description: Tracks query throughput grouped by operation type over time. Helps identify unexpected spikes in write operations or unusual read patterns.

panels.title: Read vs Write Ops/sec
panels.description: Compares read and write operation throughput over time. Shifts in the read/write ratio can reveal caching issues or unexpected write amplification.

panels.title: Errors/sec by System
panels.description: Tracks database error rates for each system over time. Spikes correlating with deployment events may indicate schema migration issues or incompatible queries.

panels.title: Errors/sec by Error Type
panels.description: Tracks database error rates grouped by error type over time. Helps distinguish transient connection errors from persistent query failures.

panels.title: Connection Count by State
panels.description: Tracks the number of database connections grouped by state (active, idle, waiting). A high ratio of waiting connections signals connection pool exhaustion.

panels.title: Pending Connection Requests
panels.description: Tracks the number of requests waiting for an available database connection over time. Non-zero values indicate the connection pool is fully utilized.

panels.title: Connection Timeout Rate
panels.description: Tracks the rate of connection acquisition timeouts over time. Any non-zero value means requests are failing because no database connection became available in time.

panels.title: Detected Database Systems
panels.description: Lists all database systems detected from span attributes, with key metrics for each. Drill down into a system to see its detailed performance breakdown.

panels.title: Top Slow Query Patterns
panels.description: Lists the slowest query patterns ranked by latency. Each row represents a normalized query signature with its p99 latency and call count for optimization prioritization.

panels.title: Slowest Collections
panels.description: Lists database collections or tables ranked by query latency. Use this to identify which data stores need indexing or schema optimization.
